steps.title: Identify the structural boundary
steps.content: After “this legendary lost work,” a new subject and independent claim emerge: “That fantasy (partially) became reality.” This marks the boundary between two separate independent clauses—the scholars' dream and what actually happened.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for independent clauses
steps.content: A period is the correct punctuation to separate two independent clauses into distinct sentences. Choice D correctly places a period after “work,” creating proper sentence boundaries. The other choices violate the rule by using a comma splice, omitting punctuation entirely, or placing a coordinating conjunction without the required comma. That matches choice D — D is correct.
